summary: @wrenfield/abitype is a typosquat of `abitype` with an import-time obfuscated remote-code dropper
details: @wrenfield/abitype impersonates the legitimate `abitype` package (publisher wevm, used by viem/wagmi). The package's `package.json` falsely declares `"repository": "wevm/abitype"` and lists the upstream maintainers `awkweb.eth` and `jxom.eth` as contributors despite being published under the unrelated `@wrenfield` scope. Both entrypoints — `dist/cjs/human-readable/runtime/utils.js` and `dist/esm/exports/index.js` — append a heavily obfuscated IIFE (obfuscator.io shape: shuffled string array, RC4-over-base64 decoder, self-defending wrapper, debugger-trap, control-flow flattening) that on `require`/`import` performs an `https.request` to a runtime-decoded URL (with 30x redirect handling), writes the response into `os.tmpdir()`, chmods it to 0o755, and spawns it as a detached child process with `stdio` rerouted and `windowsHide:true`. A self-relaunch env-var marker prevents re-entry. Any developer (Ethereum/web3 dev tooling is the explicit target audience) who installs or imports this package gets arbitrary attacker-controlled code executed on their machine, with high risk to wallet keys, mnemonics, and developer credentials present on the host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
